data.title: Gen-AI drove mobile app downloads, revenue growth in 2025: Sensor Tower
data.description:
Generative artificial intelligence apps drove mobile download growth during 2025, and for the first time, non-game apps surpassed game apps in total revenue derived from in-app purchases, according to an in-depth report by Sensor Tower.

Global in-app revenue for non-game apps increased by 21% year over year to $85.6B, while such purchases for mobile games inched up 1% to $81.8B. The report, "State of Mobile 2026," used data from Apple's (AAPL) App Store and Google's (GOOG)(GOOGL) Play Store. It did not include data from third-party purchases or revenue from advertisements.

OpenAI's (OPENAI) ChatGPT made significant gains in 2025. Its downloads increased by 148% year over year. In-app purchases (IAP) for ChatGPT surged to $3.4B in revenue, making it the third highest-grossing app in 2025. It only trailed ByteDance's (BDNCE) TikTok and Google One, a cloud storage service, in IAP revenue.

"This momentum reflects a new phase in the AI race," the report said. "Big tech companies such as Google, Microsoft (MSFT), and X are investing heavily in their AI Assistants, expanding use cases and rolling out new features in an effort to challenge the market leader, ChatGPT."

AI assistants led the way in the Gen-AI genre with the most downloads. This was led by ChatGPT, while Google's Gemini and DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) ranked second and third, respectively. xAI's (X.AI) Grok was sixth, while Meta AI (META) and Microsoft Copilot rounded out the top 10.

"In 2025, the top AI publishers, OpenAI and DeepSeek, accounted for nearly 50% of global downloads, up from just 21% in 2023," the report said. "Big tech publishers have also gained share over this period, rising from 14% in 2023 to nearly 30% in 2025. As a result, many early competitors have been crowded out, leaving roughly one-quarter of downloads for the remaining apps among the top 25 by downloads."

Image and video generation features helped propel the success of the top performers among AI apps, such as OpenAI's GPT-4o and Google's nano-banana model.

Mobile game downloads slide in 2025

Downloads of mobile gaming apps declined for the third straight year, with users downloading 50.4B gaming apps in 2025 compared to 54.3B downloads in 2024 and 57.1B in 2023. Despite the download dip, IAP revenue increased to $81.75B in 2025 from $80.7B in 2024.

The games that generated the most IAP revenue globally included Last War: Survival, Whiteout Survival, Royal Match and MONOPOLY GO!

Meanwhile, Roblox (RBLX) dominated web visits in 2025, capturing 74% of all game publisher web visits.

China's Tencent Holdings (TCEHY) ranked No. 1 among mobile game publishers by IAP revenue in 2025 at $7.5B. Scopely and Century Games ranked No. 2 and No. 3 with just over $3B, while Microsoft ranked No. 4 with $3B.

Finance app usage reflects consumer attitude

Among the finance genre, digital wallet and payment apps, such as PhonePe and PayPal (PYPL), along with cryptocurrency apps, such as Binance (BNB-USD), were among the top downloads in 2025.

Downloads for lending and personal finance apps also increased in the U.S. market.

"A modest dip in consumer sentiment in H1 2025 coincided with increased demand for Buy Now, Pay Later; Personal Finance Budgeting & Planning; and Payday Loan apps in the US," the report said.

The top five finance apps downloaded worldwide in 2025 included, in order, PhonePe, PayPal, Navi, Paytm and Google Pay.

Chinese companies dominate retail app downloads

China-based shopping apps dominated the most downloaded retail app category in 2025. Temu (PDD) ranked No. 1, followed by Shein, Amazon Shopping (AMZN), Meesho, Shopee (SE) and Alibaba (BABA). However, Amazon was still used the most among these apps.

"While Temu dominated downloads in 2025, Amazon remained the global leader in usage, averaging 685 million MAUs in Q4 2025," the report said. "Despite this lead, Temu is rapidly narrowing the gap; it surpassed 530 million MAUs following a massive 48% YoY surge in active users."

Among the food and drink category, McDonald's (MCD) was the most downloaded, followed by the food delivery service Blinkit and KFC (YUM). Meanwhile, Uber Eats' (UBER) diversified ad campaign allowed it to take market share from DoorDash (DASH).

Uber was also the most downloaded app among the travel app segment, followed by Google Maps and Waze.

Short dramas captivate video streaming

The number of video streaming app downloads increased by 39% in 2025, along with an 18% gain in total revenue.

"This resurgence is primarily fueled by the 'short drama' phenomenon," the report said. "These snackable-content platforms have dominated the rankings of the fastest-growing apps, led by Kuku TV, ReelShort, and DramaBox."

"While Asia remains the largest market by volume, contributing approximately 54% of global short drama time spent in 2025, Latin America is emerging as the fastest-growing region for engagement," the report added. "In Latin America, short dramas accounted for nearly 16% of total video entertainment time in Q4 2025."

However, in terms of revenue produced, the more traditional streaming services won. Disney+ (DIS) ranked No. 1, followed by HBO Max (WBD), WeTV, Peacock TV (CMCSA), Paramount (PSKY) and Amazon Prime Video (AMZN).

Social media app leaders  

In terms of global revenue, TikTok, YouTube and Snapchat (SNAP) topped the leaderboard. These were followed by Telegram, Kwai, X, Discord, LINE, BIGO LIVE and Meta's Instagram.

"Short-form video usage increased again across leading social apps in 2025," the report said. "While some major platforms, including TikTok, ended the year with a decline in short video's share of time spent, Meta's Instagram and Facebook more than offset this trend. Reels now accounts for a substantial share of engagement—46% of time spent on Instagram and 29% on Facebook."

Mobile app usage continues to tick up

The amount of time people peered at their smartphones and the amount of dollars spent on IAP increased in 2025.

Total IAP across iOS and Google Play climbed 10.6% to $167B. The average user spent 3.6 hours per day on mobile apps, which was a year-over-year increase of 1.1%. Consumers are also using more apps, with the average user utilizing 34 per month, or about two more apps than they did in 2024.

data.title: Lionsgate to create TV series based on English club Manchester United's history
data.description:
English football club Manchester United (MANU) has struck a deal with U.S. media production company Lionsgate (LION) to create a TV show based on the club’s history, according to a report by The Athletic on Wednesday.

The production remains in a developmental phase and has not yet been written or sold to a streaming platform, the report said, but the concept is similar in style to The Crown, the popular historical drama series on Netflix (NFLX) that chronicled the life of the British monarch Queen Elizabeth II.

Sources told the sports news outlet that an agreement has been reached, which will see Manchester United receive a guaranteed sum in the low multi-million pounds in the event the show is produced and sold.

Future royalties will be shared between United and Lionsgate, with the value growing based on the number of seasons, episodes, and the size of any deal that is agreed, the report said.

British television writer and director Jed Mercurio, known for hit UK TV series Bodyguard and Line of Duty and also a United fan since childhood, has been involved in conversations about the project.

data.title: Ormat to invest in startup to advance next-generation geothermal technology
data.description:
Ormat Technologies (ORA) +1.6% in Wednesday's trading after announcing a $25M equity investment in co-leading a $97M Series B funding round for Sage Geosystems, a startup that harnesses energy from hot rocks to generate electricity in meeting surging power demand from artificial intelligence.

Sage plans to pilot its Pressure Geothermal technology at an existing Ormat (ORA) power plant, with the goal of extracting geothermal heat from hot dry rock and accelerating the time to market for next-generation geothermal solutions.

Upon successful completion of the pilot, Ormat (ORA) said it will have the right to develop, build, own and operate geothermal power plants and energy storage projects utilizing Sage's technology.

While geothermal energy meets a small slice of U.S. power demand, the Department of Energy projects that next-generation systems have the potential to provide up to 120 GW of capacity by 2050, enough to power 90M homes.

data.title: Dr. Reddy's wins India nod for generic Ozempic
data.description:
Dr. Reddy's Laboratories (RDY) on Wednesday announced that India’s drug regulator, the Drugs Controller General of India, has issued marketing and manufacturing authorization for a generic version of Novo Nordisk’s (NVO) blockbuster GLP-1 product Ozempic.

“This would be a major opportunity,” Bloomberg reported, quoting M. V. Ramana, CEO of Dr. Reddy's Branded Markets, who added that the company is looking to sell the product at “competitive pricing” to ensure affordability.  

The Hyderabad-based drugmaker said it would launch its copycat version, indicated for Type 2 diabetes, in March when the injectable, which is made up of the GLP-1 receptor agonist semaglutide as its key ingredient, loses its market exclusivity in India.

Dr. Reddy's (RDY) plans to manufacture 12M pens in the first year of launch, CEO Erez Israeli said, adding that the company will also manufacture semaglutide and partner with other firms to market the treatment in its home country.

RDY’s off-patent version of Novo Nordisk’s (NVO) popular weight-loss drug, Wegovy, is also currently undergoing regulatory review in India. Earlier in the day, the company reported Q3 results for fiscal year 2026 exceeding Street forecasts.

data.title: Metropolitan Bank aims for 12% loan growth and modest NIM expansion in 2026 while expanding branch presence
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Metropolitan Bank Holding Corp. (MCB) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Mark DeFazio, President and CEO, stated the company is "pleased with our fourth quarter and full year 2025 performance," highlighting sustained growth in net interest margin, net interest income, deposits, and loans, as well as continued improvement in the efficiency ratio. DeFazio reported that the loan portfolio expanded by approximately $775 million in 2025, representing nearly 13% growth, with total loan originations reaching about $1.9 billion and deposit growth of roughly $1.4 billion, or about 23%. He emphasized the bank's disciplined underwriting and franchise-wide risk management culture, with plans to open two new branches in Florida in the first half of 2026 and a focus on capturing additional market share through traditional channels and new low-cost deposit initiatives. DeFazio added, "Several new initiatives will enter the market in 2026, and we expect to see early returns in the form of low-cost deposits and growth in increased fee income."
  • Daniel Dougherty, Executive VP & CFO, noted, "The loan book was essentially flat in the fourth quarter. However, we did achieve our annual target growth. In 2025, the loan book increased by $776 million or about 13%." Dougherty detailed that prepayments in the quarter were about $317 million, approximately $150 million above the trailing three-quarter run rate. He also stated, "For the fourth quarter, net interest income was $85.3 million, up more than 10% on a linked quarter basis and up almost 20% for the year."

Outlook

  • Dougherty provided 2026 guidance, stating, "We have penciled in 2 25 basis point rate cuts, one in June and one in September." The bank expects to grow loans by about $800 million, or approximately 12%, in 2026, with all planned loan growth to be funded by deposits. He added, "We expect the NIM to expand modestly over the course of 2026," and forecasted an annual NIM of about 4.10% for the year. For noninterest income, Dougherty suggested a 5% to 10% growth assumption is reasonable. Operating expenses for 2026 are expected to total about $189 million to $191 million, including $3 million in first quarter spend on the digital transformation project and $1 million in incremental premises expense.

Financial Results

  • Net interest income for the fourth quarter was $85.3 million, representing more than 10% growth from the prior quarter and nearly 20% growth for the year. Diluted EPS for the quarter was $2.77. Dougherty explained that noncore credits contributed about $4.6 million, or around $0.30 per share, stemming from above-normal prepayment penalty and deferred fee income, a securities gain of about $675,000, and insurance claim recovery along with compensation accrual adjustments. Noninterest income for the fourth quarter was $3.1 million. Noninterest expense was $44.4 million, down $1.4 million versus the prior quarter, with declines in compensation and benefits and professional fees offset by a $668,000 increase in technology costs due to the digital transformation project.

Q&A

  • Feddie Strickland, Hovde Group: Asked about potential growth in C&I after recent declines. Mark DeFazio responded, "Core C&I, I don't think you're going to see grow substantially. You'll see C&I that has a medical implication to it... But core C&I, I think we continue to manage that risk at our existing pace or slightly higher or even potentially slightly lower."
  • Strickland: Inquired about CRE concentration given recent repurchases. DeFazio replied, "I think we're -- our concentration in CRE to risk-based capital will be fairly stable going forward for sure."
  • Strickland: Questioned contributions of new branches to municipal deposit growth. DeFazio indicated New Jersey contributed, while Florida branches are expected to have significant contributions in the future.
  • David Konrad, KBW: Sought clarity on asset quality and NPAs. DeFazio explained two loans were in-market multifamily properties for sale, expecting little or no loss. He remains "cautiously optimistic" on resolving previously reserved credits in the first quarter.
  • Konrad: Asked about CET1 targets. Dougherty responded, "We expect to see that kind of trend from the current high 8s, 8.8% or so to about low 9s. And that's kind of where we feel comfortable running the institution."
  • Mark Fitzgibbon, Piper Sandler: Queried digital transformation completion. Dougherty said, "The conversion is anticipated still in the first quarter, in fact, President's Day weekend."
  • Fitzgibbon: Asked about interest recovery in NII this quarter. Dougherty replied, "No."
  • Fitzgibbon: Asked about deposit verticals driving growth. Dougherty highlighted "munis and property managers" as the largest contributors.
  • Fitzgibbon: Asked about M&A environment. DeFazio stated, "At this point, we don't see a lot of value out there in the franchises that are in our markets. So we're going to stay very close to the best here."
  • Strickland (follow-up): Asked about growth strategy regarding team lift-outs. DeFazio replied, "Likely not. It's really not part of our cultural DNA here... we're not a big fan of the team lift-outs."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was probing and focused on growth sustainability, asset quality, and cost controls, with a generally neutral to slightly positive sentiment as analysts acknowledged performance but pressed for clarity on future growth levers and risks.
  • Management maintained a confident and disciplined tone, emphasizing risk management, growth in key markets, and stable asset quality. DeFazio stated, "our results continue to show the foundational strength and stability of our business model."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment has shifted from concerns about outlier credit events toward monitoring ongoing asset quality resolution and branch expansion. Management confidence has remained steady, with more detailed guidance and explicit growth targets for 2026.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is now more specific, targeting $800 million in loan growth and a modest NIM expansion to about 4.10%. The focus has shifted from broadly discussing technology investments and AI to concrete branch expansion in key markets and new low-cost deposit initiatives. Previous quarter’s concerns about credit provisioning have been replaced by updates on resolving those credits and their expected impact. Analysts continue to press on asset quality and growth strategy but seem more reassured by current management responses and explicit financial targets. Management’s tone remains confident, with increased focus on prudent, organic growth and operational efficiency.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted that elevated loan prepayments affected fourth quarter loan growth, with $317 million in prepayments exceeding the trailing run rate. Asset quality is being closely managed, with no broad-based negative trends, but workouts on previously reserved credits are ongoing. Dougherty cautioned that allowance adjustments could occur pending final disposition of these credits. Expense pressures from digital transformation and real estate expansion are acknowledged, but management states these are planned and budgeted. Analysts raised concerns about growth in C&I, CRE concentration, and the contribution of new branches, all addressed by management as stable or improving.

Final Takeaway

Metropolitan Bank Holding Corp. closed 2025 with strong growth in loans, deposits, and net interest income, while maintaining solid asset quality and a disciplined approach to risk management. With targeted branch expansion in Florida and ongoing investment in operational efficiency, management projects 12% loan growth and modest NIM expansion for 2026, supported by a stable deposit base and prudent cost controls. The company continues to prioritize organic growth and risk mitigation, setting a clear path for sustained shareholder value into the next year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: UnitedHealth rebating profits from ACA plans to customers
data.description:
• UnitedHealth Group (UNH) CEO Stephen Hemsley said that the insurer would rebate profits made from its Affordable Care Act plans to customers as Congress continues to debate whether to extend premium tax credits.
  • "Though UnitedHealthcare is a relatively small participant in the individual ACA market, we will voluntarily eliminate and rebate our profits this year for these coverages, as Congress continues to work toward more long-term solutions," Hemsley said in prepared testimony he will make Thursday at a House Committee on Energy and Committee Subcommittee on Health hearing.
  • Executives from Cigna (CI), CVS Health (CVS), and Elevance Health (ELV) are also slated to testify.
  • During the company's Q3 2025 earnings call in late October, UnitedHealthcare Business CEO Timothy Noel said that the average ACA marketplace plan in 2026 would increase more than 25%.

data.title: The massive winter storm in the U.S. could impact Q1 restaurant earnings
data.description:
A massive Arctic-fueled winter storm is forecast to sweep from the Southern Plains in the U.S. through the Mid-South and into the Mid-Atlantic and Northeast late this week. The storm is expected to create a 2,000-mile swath of heavy snow, dangerous ice, and extremely cold temperatures that will lead to major travel and power disruptions. Major metropolitan areas in the projected path include Dallas, Austin, Oklahoma City, Nashville, Atlanta, Charlotte, Washington, D.C., Baltimore, Philadelphia, New York City, and Boston.

For investors in the retail sector, the setup is likely to display the normal pattern of major storm events, including demand spikes at grocery store chains home improvement stores, while dine-in restaurants, malls, and discretionary brick-and-mortar retail are likely to see a Q1 revenue headwind from the multi-day period of consumers sheltering inside. The southern bend of the winter storm makes it a much bigger issue for earnings reports than normal Midwest or East Coast winter storms. Just a few days of closures in impacted areas could be enough to reset earnings expectations.

Restaurant companies such as Dunkin', Darden Restaurants (DRI), Brinker International (EAT), BJ's Restaurants (BJ), Texas Roadhouse (TXRH), Shake Shack (SHAK), Sweetgreen (SG), and McDonald's (MCD) have all cited severe winter weather in the past as a drag on traffic.

data.title: From nickel dogs to $450M buyout: a timeline of Nathan's Famous
data.description:
Smithfield Foods (SFD) is buying Nathan’s Famous (NATH), the hot dog maker that started in Coney Island more than 100 years ago.

Smithfield will acquire all of Nathan’s Famous’ issued and outstanding shares for $102.00/share in cash, an enterprise value of ~$450 million.

data.title: Dollar Tree facing compressed margins, moat erosion—BNP Paribas
data.description:
Dollar Tree (DLTR) seems to be facing a collision of headwinds that threatens to not only raise operational costs but also erode its moat in the discount space, leading BNP Paribas analyst Chris Bottiglieri to turn bearish on the company with an Underperform rating.

“Inflation and de-globalization have forced DLTR to deviate from its long-term $1 moat,” Bottiglieri says, adding that declining consumable units, waning multi-price benefits, and unit elasticity offsetting pricing are all coiled to ultimately compress margins, especially as freight tailwinds unwind in 2026. Bottiglieri further warns that the combination of immigration policy, rising minimum wages, and store refreshes are likely to keep expenses elevated, defying the company’s expectations for a more balanced expense environment.

“We see a more tepid top-line environment after price increases lap and macro headwinds pickup, normalizing gross margins and less success than expected on cost cuts to weigh on DLTR’s earning algorithm,” Bottiglieri says in his note to clients.

On the demand side, the company faces impending challenges from changes to taxes, food stamp benefits, GLP-1 adoption, and agentic commerce that will further compress margins, factors that Bottiglieri believes investors are ignoring.

Consequently, BNP Paribas lowers their rating to Underperform from Neutral and cut the target price on Dollar Tree (DLTR) by 26% to $87, representing 35% downside from the prior close.

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors all share a conservative outlook for Dollar Tree (DLTR) with a consensus Hold rating. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating recently shifted from a Buy to Hold rating with a score of 3.43, reflecting some concern over the stock’s current valuation. Shares have risen 77% year-over-year, recently setting a 22-month high reflecting better-than-expected third quarter results and upbeat Q4 and upbeat 2025 outlook.

data.title: Okeanis Eco Tankers raises $130M to line up two suezmax resales
data.description:
Okeanis Eco Tankers (ECO) -3.1% in early trading Wednesday after saying it raised $130M through an offering of more than 3.6M new common shares at $36/share, using the proceeds to help finance the acquisition of two newbuilding suezmax vessels currently under construction at the Daehan Shipbuilding shipyard for a combined acquisition price of $198.6M.

Each vessel is a sister vessel to the two vessels delivered to Okeanis (ECO) earlier this month, and are expected to be delivered from the shipyard during Q2 2026.

In a commercial update released Tuesday, Okeanis (ECO) said its fleet achieved an average time-charter equivalent rate of $75.4K/day in Q4 2025, compared with $46.6K/day in Q3, and the average TCE rate for the company's eight VLCCs jumped to $91.3K/day in Q4 and to $110.1K/day so far in 2026, with 37% of available Q1 spot days already booked.

Okeanis (ECO) was easily the top performer among 27 mostly U.S.-traded stocks in 2025 with a 57% share gain, according to TradeWindsNews.com.
